text.title: Recommended test tools per layer
text.summary: The methodology is prescriptive on "must exist", NOT on framework choice. These are recommendations the team can override per language / stack.

list.item: Unit tests (Python) — pytest. (Alt: unittest if pytest is unavailable.)
Unit tests (TypeScript / JavaScript) — vitest or jest.
API tests (Python) — pytest + httpx or DRF APIClient.
API tests (any language) — direct HTTP calls in the unit-test framework.
E2E user-facing — Playwright. (Cypress is an acceptable alternative; Selenium is not.)
Visual regression — Playwright screenshot diff + reg-cli or Chromatic.
Load / perf — k6 or Locust; outside per-feature scope, governed at project-spec/non-functional.md.

rule.statement: The methodology MUST NOT block a feature solely because a different framework was chosen for the same gate.
rule.rationale: The gate is "tests exist and pass". Framework politics is a separate decision recorded in project-spec/decisions/.

antipattern.description: Using Postman collections as the API test gate.
antipattern.reason: Postman collections do not run in CI without extra plumbing; they drift from the codebase. API tests must live with the code and run in CI.
